knowledge_base.emergency_dashboard_purpose: The Emergency Dashboard provides a real-time overview of all active admissions in the Emergency Department. It displays patient status, medical alerts, and assigned clinicians to ensure rapid response and coordination.
knowledge_base.nurse_dashboard_purpose: The Nurse Dashboard (Intake Station) is used for identifying new arrivals via biometrics or manual entry, provisioning emergency wristbands, and initializing patient records before full admission.
knowledge_base.emergency_id_system: The Emergency ID system generates unique identifiers (e.g., EM-XXXXX) for unidentified or trauma patients. These IDs allow clinical staff to track treatment and append notes until the patient's real identity is verified via biometrics or documentation.
knowledge_base.patient_profile_content: A patient profile includes the Emergency ID, Name (if known), Age, Blood Group, known Allergies, Chronic Conditions, Current Medications, and a timeline of recent status changes.
knowledge_base.workflow_search: To search for a patient: 1. Navigate to the 'Search Patient' tab. 2. Enter the Patient ID or Name in the search field. 3. View the patient's record and clinical history.
knowledge_base.workflow_discharge: To discharge a patient: 1. Locate the patient card on the Emergency Dashboard. 2. Click the 'Discharge' button. 3. Provide discharge notes and confirm. The record will be moved to the archival logs.
knowledge_base.workflow_manual_entry: To manually add a patient: 1. Click the 'Manual Entry' button on the Emergency Dashboard. 2. Fill in the required fields (Name defaults to 'Unknown Patient' if left blank). 3. Submit to create a temporary Emergency ID record.
knowledge_base.term_severe: Severe Condition: Indicates a patient with significant injuries or illnesses requiring immediate clinical intervention and continuous monitoring.
knowledge_base.term_emergency: Emergency Condition: Indicates a patient requiring urgent care but whose vitals are currently stable compared to critical cases.
knowledge_base.term_normal: Normal Condition: Indicates a patient requiring routine treatment or observation who is not in immediate distress.
knowledge_base.term_admission_time: Admission Time: The exact timestamp when the patient was first checked into the system, used to track length of stay and priority.
knowledge_base.term_emergency_id: Emergency ID: A unique temporary identifier used to manage trauma patients until their identity is definitively established.
